strategic_profile.executive_summary: Portland State University (PSU) is a public research university located in downtown Portland, Oregon, known for its deep integration with the city and its motto, 'Let knowledge serve the city.' As an inclusive, non-selective institution and an Asian American and Native American Pacific Islander-Serving Institution (AANAPISI), it caters heavily to commuter, non-traditional, and first-generation students. The university emphasizes social mobility, applied urban research, and sustainability.

analyst_takeaways.insight: With an acceptance rate exceeding 90%, PSU is highly accessible and relies primarily on GPA for admission decisions.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students with a 2.5+ GPA have a very high likelihood of admission, making it a reliable safety school.

analyst_takeaways.insight: 48% of students are first-generation, and the campus has a high percentage of commuter and non-traditional students.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The social environment is deeply integrated into Portland rather than focused on traditional, insular campus life.

analyst_takeaways.insight: 100% of students must complete a community-based capstone project before graduation.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Provides strong hands-on, civic-minded learning opportunities that connect students with local employers.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Urban and progressive, lacking a traditional insular campus bubble. Highly commuter-driven with active student engagement in local activism, social justice, and environmental sustainability.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Excellent public transit connectivity (light rail, streetcar, and bus networks) mitigates the lack of abundant campus parking and supports the heavy commuter population.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Carnegie R2 (High Research Activity) institution with significant applied research in urban planning, public affairs, and sustainability.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test scores are not considered for admission, even if submitted. Optional but not required.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Portland State University does not have a policy of placing students on a waiting list.
Data sourced from 2023-2024 Common Data Set.

gpa.notes: Average high school GPA is 3.36. 19.9% had a GPA between 3.75 and 3.99.

testing.policy_note: Test-blind for admissions. 2.15% submitted SAT scores and 2.97% submitted ACT scores, though they were not used for admission.

colleges.name: Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking career-focused tech programs with strong connections to local employers like Intel and a flexible path to declaring.
colleges.housing_profile: Primarily urban commuter with optional university housing.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Must submit an 'Opt-In' form before week 8 to take upper-division courses, conditional upon completing lower-division prerequisites with a C or better.
majors.notes: Upon entering upper-division courses, differential tuition rates apply.

colleges.name: School of Social Work
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students focused on urban community engagement, social justice, and systemic support roles.

majors.name: Social Work
majors.special_requirements: Must apply to the BSW program by March 1 of sophomore year for fall term upper-division admission.
majors.notes: The program accepts around 90 students per academic year.

application_deadlines.notes: Rolling admissions process for general admission; August 1 is the deadline for fall term.

supplemental_requirements.details: General admission does not require essays, but Honors College applicants must complete supplemental essays. Awards and admission are based primarily on these essays.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Admission is heavily numerical. High school GPAs over 2.50 generally ensure admission; holistic review comes into play mainly for students with a GPA of 2.49 or lower, or those applying to the Honors College.

major_selection_tactics: Apply undeclared or pre-major without penalty, as programs like Computer Science require internal 'opt-in' processes regardless of initial admission.
Focus on passing prerequisite filters rather than stressing initial major declaration.
Aim for the Honors College using strong essays if looking for a more traditional, highly engaged academic cohort.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: General admission is not major-dependent. However, alternate plans are essential for upper-division entry into impacted programs like Nursing or Social Work.

scholarships.name: The Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full cost of tuition and fees. Highly competitive.

scholarships.name: Four Years Free
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition for eligible Pell Grant-qualifying Oregon residents with a 3.4+ GPA.

scholarships.name: Honors Laurels Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Tuition remission based on application essays for the Honors College.

scholarships.name: Western Undergraduate Exchange (WUE)
scholarships.benefits: Reduces out-of-state tuition to 150% of the resident rate for students from participating Western states.

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards college credit for scores of 3, 4, or 5 depending on the subject.
exceptions: Specific course equivalencies apply based on admission year and departmental charts.
ap_policy.usage: Used for course credit and placement.

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for creditable scores on IB exams.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum 124 credits accepted from junior/community colleges. Maximum 12 credits for vocational/technical courses.

what_it_takes: Complete 15 units of required college preparatory coursework.
Test scores and essays are virtually irrelevant for standard admission.
common_activities: Urban activism and social justice programs
Off-campus internships and full-time or part-time employment
essay_tips: Focus on civic engagement, overcoming adversity, and alignment with the 'Let knowledge serve the city' ethos.
red_flags: Dropping below a 2.5 GPA will trigger manual review.
Failing to submit required high school transcripts.
insights: Many students are non-traditional or transfer students (community college transfers), giving the campus an older, more mature demographic.
